The '''Mission to Corellia''' took place in [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]].

==Background==
Having received reports about [[Sith cult/Legends|cult]] activity on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], [[Kyle Katarn]] and [[Jaden Korr]] were sent to [[Coronet City/Legends|Coronet]] to investigate whether the locals were just being paranoid or whether the [[Disciples of Ragnos]] were involved.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

==The mission==
Upon receiving a distress call from a [[Hovertrain/Legends|maglev train]] informing that someone was trying to blow it up, Katarn and Korr learned that [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] were boarding the train, using [[Hover Transport TC9|cargo ships]] as landing vessels. Katarn dropped off Korr on the train to stop it before it crashed into a building, while Katarn himself was to engage the cargo ships in the ''[[Raven's Claw]]''.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

Trying to get to the locomotive of the train, Korr had to jump from one freight car to another, all the while fighting enemies. He soon discovered that the train was indeed rigged but managed to disarm the bomb. Korr encountered several mercenaries, including [[J'aahn]] and [[Hokk]], killing them all,{{Fact}} and even dueled a few [[Sith cultist/Legends|Sith Cultists]]. Eventually, Korr managed to get to the locomotive and stop the train, while Katarn successfully destroyed the mercenaries' cargo ships.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

==Behind the scenes==
Some dialogue on this level of ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'' identifies J'aahn as a cultist, a reference to ''Soldier of Fortune'', another game by Raven Software. Both games contain an exchange of dialogue which involves characters with the names John and Hawk, and refers to "Station 6C." (This can be confused for 6E) In ''Jedi Academy'', the spelling is changed to "J'aahn" and "Hokk."

In some cases, the lightning in the background weather of the level can hit the player and enemies.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'' {{1st}}
